{
  "project": "Metro Integrated Planning System (MMS)",
  "version": "0.3.0",
  "lastUpdated": "2025-12-16",
  "product": {
    "entry": "apps/canvas",
    "description": "Production React app with semantic zoom (Vite + React 18 + R3F + BlueprintJS 6)"
  },
  "reference": {
    "entry": "design/reference/semantic-zoom-poc.html",
    "description": "Standalone POC for visual reference only"
  },
  "priorityQueue": {
    "description": "Features ordered by execution priority. Execute in order, respecting blockedBy dependencies.",
    "note": "Priority 0 = immediate, Priority 1 = high, Priority 2 = after dependencies"
  },
  "features": [
    {
      "id": "URGENT-001",
      "category": "harness",
      "priority": 0,
      "description": "EMERGENCIAL: Migrar uso de React hooks para external stores em todo o projeto",
      "steps": [
        "Auditar todo o código em apps/canvas para identificar TODOS os usos de useState, useRef, useCallback, useMemo que gerenciam estado compartilhado ou estado crítico de renderização",
        "Documentar cada uso encontrado com: arquivo, linha, propósito, e se pode ser migrado para external store",
        "Priorizar migração de estados que causam problemas de stale closures (como cameraPosRef workaround em InfiniteCanvas)",
        "Priorizar migração de estados que são lidos em loops de animação (useFrame, requestAnimationFrame)",
        "Criar ou usar external store pattern (useSyncExternalStore) para CADA estado identificado que não seja puramente local ao componente",
        "React hooks SÓ devem ser usados quando não houver alternativa (ex: useEffect para lifecycle, useState para estado puramente local de UI como hover)",
        "Garantir que NENHUM estado crítico de renderização (zoom, pan, camera position, animation state) use React hooks",
        "Atualizar CLAUDE.md com regra: 'NUNCA usar React hooks para estado compartilhado ou crítico de renderização - usar external stores (useSyncExternalStore)'",
        "Criar checklist de code review para garantir que futuras alterações não introduzam React hooks onde external stores devem ser usados",
        "Testar todas as interações após migração: pan, zoom, momentum, animações, navegação sidebar/timeline"
      ],
      "passes": false,
      "blockedBy": null,
      "notes": "EMERGENCIAL - React hooks causam muitos problemas em aplicações WebGL: stale closures em animation loops, batching de estado causando jumps/glitches, re-renders desnecessários. External stores (useSyncExternalStore) resolvem todos esses problemas pois o estado é lido sincronamente sem passar pelo ciclo de render do React."
    },
    {
      "id": "PERF-001",
      "category": "performance",
      "priority": 0,
      "description": "Adicionar ferramenta de desenvolvimento para métricas de performance WebGL em tempo real + logging detalhado",
      "steps": [
        "Pesquisar e avaliar ferramentas disponíveis: r3f-perf (https://github.com/utsuboco/r3f-perf), stats-gl (https://github.com/RenaudRohlinger/stats-gl), stats.js (https://github.com/mrdoob/stats.js), three-performance-panel",
        "r3f-perf é a recomendada para R3F pois mostra: FPS, frame time (MS), memory (MB), GPU time, draw calls, triangles, textures, shaders, geometries, e permite custom panels",
        "stats-gl é alternativa que suporta WebGL2 EXT_disjoint_timer_query_webgl2 para métricas precisas de GPU",
        "Instalar a ferramenta escolhida via bun add (NUNCA editar package.json manualmente)",
        "Criar componente PerfMonitor que renderiza o painel de métricas APENAS em desenvolvimento (process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development' ou flag)",
        "Posicionar o painel em local que não interfira na UI principal (canto superior direito ou inferior esquerdo)",
        "O painel DEVE mostrar no mínimo: FPS (frames por segundo), MS (milliseconds por frame), MB (memory em megabytes), GPU time se disponível, draw calls, triangles count",
        "O painel DEVE ser visível tanto para AI (via screenshot do Playwright MCP) quanto para humanos inspecionando manualmente no browser",
        "Adicionar toggle para mostrar/esconder o painel (tecla 'P' ou botão na navbar)",
        "Documentar como interpretar as métricas e quais valores são aceitáveis para 60fps (MS < 16.67ms, FPS >= 60)",
        "Adicionar métricas customizadas se necessário: LOD transition time, navigation animation time, momentum calculation time",
        "LOGGING DETALHADO - Criar sistema de logging de performance que capture métricas ao longo do tempo para análise posterior",
        "LOGGING DETALHADO - O log deve capturar a cada N frames (configurável, ex: a cada 60 frames = 1 segundo): timestamp, FPS médio, FPS mínimo, FPS máximo, MS médio, MS máximo, memory atual, draw calls, triangles, evento atual (idle, panning, zooming, animating, LOD transition)",
        "LOGGING DETALHADO - Criar buffer circular em memória para armazenar últimos X segundos de métricas (ex: últimos 60 segundos)",
        "LOGGING DETALHADO - Expor função global window.__PERF_LOG__ ou similar para que AI e humanos possam acessar o log via console do browser",
        "LOGGING DETALHADO - Formato do log deve ser JSON estruturado para fácil parsing: { timestamp, fps: {avg, min, max}, ms: {avg, min, max}, memory, drawCalls, triangles, event, duration }",
        "LOGGING DETALHADO - Adicionar comando no console para exportar log completo: window.__PERF_EXPORT__() que retorna array de todas as entradas",
        "LOGGING DETALHADO - Adicionar comando para iniciar/parar gravação de sessão específica: window.__PERF_START_SESSION__(name), window.__PERF_STOP_SESSION__() que retorna métricas agregadas da sessão",
        "LOGGING DETALHADO - Sessões devem calcular estatísticas agregadas: FPS médio da sessão, percentil 1% (worst case), percentil 99%, tempo total, contagem de frames abaixo de 30fps, contagem de frames abaixo de 60fps",
        "LOGGING DETALHADO - Permitir que AI execute via Playwright: browser_evaluate para chamar window.__PERF_START_SESSION__('test'), realizar interações, chamar window.__PERF_STOP_SESSION__() e analisar resultados",
        "LOGGING DETALHADO - Log deve ser acessível mesmo quando painel visual está escondido",
        "LOGGING DETALHADO - Documentar todos os comandos disponíveis e formato dos dados retornados em docs/PERFORMANCE_LOGGING.md"
      ],
      "passes": false,
      "blockedBy": null,
      "notes": "Ferramentas pesquisadas: r3f-perf (recomendada para R3F, mostra FPS/MS/MB/GPU/draw calls/triangles/textures/shaders), stats-gl (WebGL2 GPU timing), stats.js (básico FPS/MS/MB), three-performance-panel (lightweight). IMPORTANTE: Além do painel visual, deve haver sistema de LOGGING DETALHADO que permita análise posterior das métricas. O log deve ser acessível via console (window.__PERF_LOG__, window.__PERF_EXPORT__(), window.__PERF_START_SESSION__/STOP_SESSION__) para que tanto AI (via browser_evaluate) quanto humanos possam analisar performance detalhadamente, não apenas visualmente."
    },
    {
      "id": "HARNESS-003",
      "category": "harness",
      "priority": 1,
      "description": "Atualizar protocolo de testes visuais do Harness para incluir métricas de performance (visual + logging)",
      "steps": [
        "Modificar docs/QA_PROTOCOL.md para incluir verificação obrigatória de métricas de performance em CADA teste visual",
        "Definir métricas obrigatórias a verificar: FPS (mínimo 60 em idle, mínimo 30 durante interações intensas), MS (máximo 16.67ms em idle, máximo 33ms durante interações), Memory (não deve crescer continuamente durante uso normal)",
        "Definir métricas opcionais mas recomendadas: draw calls (quanto menor melhor), triangles count, GPU time se disponível",
        "Criar checklist de performance para cada tipo de teste: idle state, pan interaction, zoom interaction, LOD transition, navigation animation",
        "Atualizar testingProtocol em features.json para incluir step de verificação de métricas",
        "Documentar como a AI deve interpretar o painel de métricas no screenshot: identificar valores, comparar com thresholds, reportar pass/fail",
        "Definir formato de report de performance visual: 'PERF: FPS=60, MS=12.5, MB=45.2, DrawCalls=3 - PASS' ou 'PERF: FPS=45, MS=22.1 - FAIL (FPS abaixo de 60)'",
        "Adicionar regra: se métricas de performance não estiverem visíveis no screenshot, o teste DEVE falhar e solicitar que PERF-001 seja implementado primeiro",
        "LOGGING - Protocolo de teste DEVE usar o sistema de logging de PERF-001 para análise detalhada, não apenas visual",
        "LOGGING - Para cada teste de performance, AI deve executar via browser_evaluate: (1) window.__PERF_START_SESSION__('nome_do_teste'), (2) realizar interações do teste, (3) window.__PERF_STOP_SESSION__() para obter métricas agregadas",
        "LOGGING - Definir critérios de aprovação baseados no log: FPS médio >= 60 em idle, FPS médio >= 30 em interação, percentil 1% (worst case) >= 20fps, zero frames abaixo de 10fps",
        "LOGGING - Formato de report de log: 'PERF_LOG[test_name]: avgFPS=58.2, p1FPS=32.1, p99FPS=60.0, framesBelow30=12, framesBelow60=45, duration=5.2s - PASS/FAIL'",
        "LOGGING - Documentar quando usar análise visual (quick check) vs análise de log (detailed check): quick para features simples, detailed para features de interação/animação",
        "LOGGING - Criar template de teste de performance que combine visual + log para máxima cobertura",
        "Testar o novo protocolo com uma feature existente para validar que funciona (testar tanto visual quanto logging)"
      ],
      "passes": false,
      "blockedBy": "PERF-001",
      "notes": "O Harness precisa avaliar performance automaticamente durante testes visuais usando DUAS abordagens complementares: (1) VISUAL - ler métricas do painel no screenshot para quick check, (2) LOGGING - usar window.__PERF_START_SESSION__/STOP_SESSION__ via browser_evaluate para análise detalhada com estatísticas agregadas. Métricas de referência: 60fps = 16.67ms/frame, 30fps = 33.33ms/frame. O log permite análise de percentis (p1, p99), contagem de frames ruins, e duração total - informações que o visual sozinho não fornece."
    },
    {
      "id": "HARNESS-004",
      "category": "harness",
      "priority": 1,
      "description": "Migrar tarefas POC do archive para fluxo normal de features pendentes",
      "steps": [
        "As tarefas F016-POC até F028-POC NÃO são lixo ou draft - são features válidas que devem ser implementadas",
        "Mover TODAS as tarefas com sufixo -POC do archive.deferredFeatures para a lista principal de features",
        "Remover o sufixo -POC do ID de cada tarefa migrada (F016-POC vira F016, etc)",
        "Atribuir priority adequada a cada tarefa migrada baseado na importância: acessibilidade (F021, F022) = priority 2, interação (F018, F019, F020) = priority 2, keyboard shortcuts (F027, F028) = priority 3, features avançadas (F023, F025, F026) = priority 3",
        "Manter F016 (Shift+Scroll vertical pan) e F017 (Ctrl+Scroll horizontal pan) com priority 2 pois melhoram navegação",
        "F024-POC (Canvas maintains 60fps) pode ser removida pois F116 já cobre isso",
        "Atualizar blockedBy de cada tarefa migrada para refletir dependências corretas",
        "Atualizar notes de cada tarefa para remover referências a 'POC' ou 'deferred' e adicionar contexto relevante",
        "Verificar que archive.deferredFeatures fica vazio ou apenas com F117 (que é realmente archived)",
        "Atualizar CLAUDE.md se necessário para refletir que POC features agora são features normais"
      ],
      "passes": false,
      "blockedBy": null,
      "notes": "As tarefas F016-F028 foram originalmente criadas durante o POC mas são features válidas e necessárias para o produto final. Elas não devem ficar escondidas no archive como 'deferred' - devem estar no fluxo normal de trabalho para serem implementadas. O POC serviu para validar o conceito, mas as features identificadas são reais."
    },
    {
      "id": "HARNESS-001",
      "category": "harness",
      "priority": 0,
      "description": "Configure Playwright MCP for headless without focus stealing",
      "steps": [
        "Research Playwright headless configuration options",
        "Update MCP settings for headless mode",
        "Test that browser windows don't steal focus during visual tests",
        "Document configuration in CLAUDE.md"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": null,
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Playwright MCP configured with --headless flag in .mcp.json. Deny permissions added to block access to personal directories."
    },
    {
      "id": "QA-PLAN-001",
      "category": "harness",
      "priority": 0,
      "description": "Plan QA testing protocol with deep thinking (planning mode)",
      "steps": [
        "Enter planning mode with deep thinking enabled",
        "Research QA best practices for canvas/WebGL applications",
        "Design protocol to enforce QA tests AFTER visual tests",
        "Define QA checklist based on UX-AUDIT-001 discoveries",
        "Update harness hooks to enforce QA step",
        "Document in docs/QA_PROTOCOL.md"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "HARNESS-001",
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Created docs/QA_PROTOCOL.md with WebGL testing protocol. Updated session-start.sh and end-of-turn-check.sh hooks. Protocol includes: typecheck, visual test, console check, interaction test, performance check sequence."
    },
    {
      "id": "UX-AUDIT-001",
      "category": "research",
      "priority": 1,
      "description": "UX Best Practices Deep Research & Documentation",
      "steps": [
        "Enter planning mode with deep thinking",
        "Study UI patterns: Canva, Figma, Google Maps, iOS, Android",
        "Document all mouse events (click, dblclick, drag, hover, wheel, context)",
        "Document all touch events (tap, pinch, swipe, long-press)",
        "Research cross-platform differences (macOS/Windows/Linux scroll)",
        "Research hardware differences (trackpad vs mouse)",
        "Create docs/UX_RESEARCH.md with all findings",
        "Update harness to preserve context across sessions"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "HARNESS-001",
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Created docs/UX_RESEARCH.md with comprehensive research on: Figma/Google Maps/iOS patterns, mouse/touch/pointer events, wheel normalization (Facebook pattern), momentum physics, cross-platform scroll differences, accessibility requirements. Includes implementation recommendations for F118-F121."
    },
    {
      "id": "F118",
      "category": "ux",
      "priority": 2,
      "description": "Momentum/inertia panning (velocity-based drag)",
      "steps": [
        "Track drag velocity during mouse movement",
        "On mouse release, continue panning with velocity",
        "Apply friction to gradually stop",
        "Test on macOS, Windows (if available)",
        "Match Google Maps/iOS feel"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "UX-AUDIT-001",
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Implemented with velocity tracking during drag, friction-based animation loop (0.93 friction), and momentum stops at velocity < 0.5. Tested via Playwright MCP 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "F119",
      "category": "ux",
      "priority": 2,
      "description": "Smooth zoom UX (iOS/Google Maps feel)",
      "steps": [
        "Add easing to zoom transitions",
        "Implement zoom momentum",
        "Add pinch-to-zoom support for touch devices",
        "Smooth out zoom steps",
        "Test across different input devices"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "UX-AUDIT-001",
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Implemented quadOut easing for zoom animations (250ms), wheel event accumulation for trackpad smoothing, pinch-to-zoom support via Pointer Events API, animated zoom for +/- buttons and Reset. Tested via Playwright MCP 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "F120",
      "category": "ux",
      "priority": 2,
      "description": "Double-click to zoom in (BUG FIX)",
      "steps": [
        "Add dblclick event handler to canvas",
        "Calculate click position in world coordinates",
        "Zoom 2x centered on click position",
        "Animate zoom transition smoothly",
        "Respect MAX_ZOOM limit"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "UX-AUDIT-001",
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Implemented with 350ms cubicOut animation. Zooms 2x toward click point, respects MAX_ZOOM (5.0x). Tested via Playwright MCP 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "F121",
      "category": "ux",
      "priority": 2,
      "description": "Cross-platform scroll/input sensitivity normalization",
      "steps": [
        "Research deltaY values across browsers/OS",
        "Normalize scroll sensitivity for macOS/Windows/Linux",
        "Handle trackpad vs mouse differences",
        "Implement adaptive sensitivity based on input type",
        "Test on multiple platforms"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "UX-AUDIT-001",
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Implemented deltaMode normalization (LINE/PAGE to pixels), trackpad vs mouse detection by event frequency, adaptive sensitivity (mouse: 0.003, trackpad: 0.001), zoom delta clamping (±0.5 per event). Tested via Playwright MCP 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "F122",
      "category": "ux",
      "priority": 1,
      "description": "BUG FIX: Elastic/jump effect when dragging canvas",
      "steps": [
        "Investigate elastic/jump behavior during drag",
        "Check momentum calculation and velocity samples",
        "Review camera position clamping logic",
        "Test on slow and fast drags",
        "Ensure smooth continuous pan without jumps"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": null,
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Root cause: React state batching caused stale cameraPos when starting new drag during momentum. Fix: Added cameraPosRef for synchronous position tracking. handlePointerDown now uses ref instead of state. All setCameraPos calls sync to ref via updateCameraPos(). Tested with rapid drag interruptions via Playwright MCP 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "F116",
      "category": "performance",
      "priority": 3,
      "description": "Performance optimization (60fps)",
      "steps": [
        "Profile with Chrome DevTools",
        "Maintain 60fps during pan/zoom",
        "No memory leaks on extended use",
        "InstancedMesh optimizations",
        "Frustum culling for off-screen tasks"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F110",
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Optimized MesoLayer RowBackgrounds from 50 individual meshes to 1 InstancedMesh. All layers now use InstancedMesh for optimal draw calls. MicroLayer already had frustum culling and optimized rendering. Visual tests pass at all LOD levels (MACRO/MESO/MICRO). No console errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "F123",
      "category": "ux",
      "priority": 3,
      "description": "Minimap navigation (bird's eye view)",
      "steps": [
        "Research minimap patterns in design tools (Figma, Canva) and games",
        "Create minimap component showing entire canvas at small scale",
        "Show viewport rectangle indicating current view position",
        "Allow click/drag on minimap to navigate",
        "Position in corner (bottom-right recommended)",
        "Add toggle to show/hide minimap"
      ],
      "passes": false,
      "blockedBy": "F110",
      "notes": "FEATURE REQUEST: Add minimap like games/design tools to help users locate themselves in the larger canvas. Shows entire 10-year timeline + 50 trains at small scale with viewport indicator."
    },
    {
      "id": "TECH-DEBT-001",
      "category": "harness",
      "priority": 0,
      "description": "Migrate camera state to @mms/toolkit state-machine (replace React hooks) - RELACIONADO A URGENT-001",
      "steps": [
        "✅ Research: @mms/toolkit already uses useSyncExternalStore (ideal for WebGL)",
        "✅ Research: Store.create() provides external store with subscribe/getState/dispatch",
        "✅ Research: useMachine() hook returns [state, actions] tuple",
        "NOTA: Esta tarefa está relacionada a URGENT-001 - a migração de React hooks para external stores é a mesma iniciativa",
        "NOTA: @mms/toolkit já tem o pattern ideal implementado, só precisa ser usado em apps/canvas",
        "Create viewport slice in @mms/toolkit for camera state: zoom (number), panX (number), panY (number), lod ('MACRO' | 'MESO' | 'MICRO'), cameraPos ({x, y}), velocity ({x, y}), isAnimating (boolean), animationTarget (optional)",
        "Create canvas machine with viewport slice + future slices: selection (selectedTaskId), hover (hoveredTaskId), navigation (navigationTarget)",
        "Migrate InfiniteCanvas from useState/useRef to useMachine('viewport') - substituir TODOS os useState relacionados a camera/viewport",
        "Remove cameraPosRef workaround - external store resolve o problema de stale closure automaticamente pois o estado é lido sincronamente",
        "Migrate animation loops (momentum, zoom animation) para usar o store diretamente sem passar pelo React render cycle",
        "Test: rapid drag interruptions (o bug que F122 corrigiu com workaround), momentum physics, zoom animations, navigation animations",
        "Verificar que re-renders do React NÃO afetam mais as animações - o canvas deve atualizar independente do React"
      ],
      "passes": false,
      "blockedBy": null,
      "notes": "IN PROGRESS: @mms/toolkit já tem o pattern ideal (useSyncExternalStore). Esta tarefa é parte da iniciativa URGENT-001 de eliminar React hooks para estado crítico. O workaround cameraPosRef em InfiniteCanvas (adicionado em F122) é sintoma do problema que external stores resolvem definitivamente."
    },
    {
      "id": "HARNESS-002",
      "category": "harness",
      "priority": 0,
      "description": "Reduce harness file sizes (archive completed features)",
      "steps": [
        "Analyze features.json size and identify bloat",
        "Move ALL completed features (passes:true) to separate archive file",
        "Keep only pending features in main features.json",
        "Update harness hooks to read from both files if needed",
        "Ensure context is preserved for AI agents"
      ],
      "passes": false,
      "blockedBy": null,
      "notes": "features.json is getting large with completed features that may not add value to current context."
    },
    {
      "id": "F124",
      "category": "ux",
      "priority": 2,
      "description": "Sidebar and Timeline interactive navigation",
      "steps": [
        "Research best UX patterns for scrollable sidebars (Figma, VS Code, design tools)",
        "Add clickable train IDs in sidebar to navigate directly to that train",
        "Add clickable dates in timeline header to navigate to that time",
        "Implement search/filter functionality to jump to specific train",
        "Add keyboard shortcut for quick navigation (Cmd+G/Ctrl+G 'Go to...')",
        "Consider dropdown or autocomplete for direct navigation",
        "Ensure smooth animated transition when navigating"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": null,
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Implemented clickable train IDs in sidebar and clickable date labels in timeline. Navigation uses smooth 350ms animation. Hover effects with background highlight. Tooltips show 'Click to navigate to...'. Search/filter and keyboard shortcuts deferred to future enhancement. Tested via Playwright MCP 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "F001",
      "category": "core",
      "description": "Canvas renders and initializes without errors",
      "steps": [
        "Open design/reference/semantic-zoom-poc.html in browser",
        "Canvas displays with header, sidebar, and main area",
        "No JavaScript errors in console",
        "Mock data generates 50 trains with 10 years of tasks"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": null,
      "notes": "POC validated semantic zoom concept. Production app: apps/canvas"
    },
    {
      "id": "F002",
      "category": "navigation",
      "description": "Pan navigation with drag",
      "steps": [
        "Click and drag on canvas to pan",
        "Cursor changes to grabbing while dragging",
        "Pan works in all directions (X and Y)",
        "Sidebar and timeline header sync with pan position"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F001",
      "notes": "Implemented with mousedown/mousemove/mouseup events"
    },
    {
      "id": "F003",
      "category": "navigation",
      "description": "Zoom navigation with scroll wheel",
      "steps": [
        "Scroll up to zoom in, scroll down to zoom out",
        "Zoom centers on mouse cursor position",
        "Zoom respects MIN_ZOOM (0.1x) and MAX_ZOOM (5x) limits",
        "Zoom level indicator updates in header"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F001",
      "notes": "Zoom toward mouse position for natural feel"
    },
    {
      "id": "F004",
      "category": "navigation",
      "description": "Double-click zoom",
      "steps": [
        "Double-click zooms in 2x at click location",
        "Works at any current zoom level (respects max)",
        "Animation is smooth (no jarring jump)"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F003",
      "notes": "Quick zoom in gesture"
    },
    {
      "id": "F005",
      "category": "navigation",
      "description": "Zoom button controls",
      "steps": [
        "Plus button zooms in by 1.5x",
        "Minus button zooms out by 1.5x",
        "Reset button returns to zoom 1.0 and pan origin",
        "Buttons zoom toward center of viewport"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F003",
      "notes": "Alternative for users without scroll wheel"
    },
    {
      "id": "F006",
      "category": "lod",
      "description": "Macro layer renders at zoom < 0.5x",
      "steps": [
        "Zoom out below 0.5x to trigger Macro view",
        "Heatmap displays with Yellow-Orange-Red gradient",
        "Cell density reflects task count per month/train",
        "LOD indicator shows 'MACRO' with yellow badge"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F003",
      "notes": "Strategic 10-20 year overview"
    },
    {
      "id": "F007",
      "category": "lod",
      "description": "Meso layer renders at zoom 0.5x-2x",

    12→1. **URGENT-001** (Priority 0 - EMERGENCIAL)
    13→   - Migrar uso de React hooks para external stores em todo o projeto
    14→   - React hooks causam problemas em WebGL: stale closures, batching, re-renders
    15→   - External stores (useSyncExternalStore) resolvem todos esses problemas
    16→   - Auditar código, documentar usos, migrar estados críticos
    17→   - Atualizar CLAUDE.md com regra contra React hooks para estado crítico

    19→2. **PERF-001** (Priority 0)

    21→   - Ferramentas pesquisadas: r3f-perf (recomendada), stats-gl, stats.js
    22→   - Painel deve mostrar: FPS, MS, MB, GPU time, draw calls, triangles
    23→   - Visível para AI (via screenshot) e humanos (no browser)

    26→     - Buffer circular em memória para últimos 60s de métricas
    27→     - window.__PERF_LOG__ para acessar log via console

   178→## Session: 2025-12-16 (F122 - Fix elastic/jump drag bug) - COMPLETE ✅
   179→
   180→### F122: Fix elastic/jump effect when dragging - COMPLETE
   181→
   182→**Problem:**
   183→Canvas felt elastic or jumped when starting a new drag during/after momentum animation.
   184→
   185→**Root Cause Analysis:**
   186→React state updates are batched/async. When user starts a new drag while momentum is running:
   187→1. `handlePointerDown` captures `cameraPos` from React state
   188→2. But React state hasn't been updated yet with latest momentum position
   189→3. New drag starts from stale position → canvas "jumps" back
   190→
   191→**Solution Implemented:**
   192→1. Added `cameraPosRef` to track camera position synchronously via useRef
   193→2. Created `updateCameraPos()` helper that updates BOTH ref and state
   194→3. Updated all `setCameraPos()` calls to use `updateCameraPos()`:
   195→   - `handleCameraInitialized`
   196→   - `animateZoomTo` (zoom animation loop)
   197→   - `startMomentum` (momentum animation loop)
   198→   - `handlePinchZoom`
   199→   - `handlePointerMove` (pan movement)
   200→   - `handleDoubleClick` (double-click zoom animation)
   201→4. Changed `handlePointerDown` to read from `cameraPosRef.current` instead of `cameraPos` state
   202→

   252→### F121: Cross-platform scroll/input normalization - COMPLETE
   253→
   254→**Objective:**
   255→Normalize scroll sensitivity across macOS/Windows/Linux and trackpad/mouse.
   256→
   257→**Implementation:**
   258→
   259→1. **deltaMode Normalization:**
   260→   - LINE mode (deltaMode=1): multiply by LINE_HEIGHT (40px)
   261→   - PAGE mode (deltaMode=2): multiply by PAGE_HEIGHT (800px)
   262→   - PIXEL mode (deltaMode=0): use directly
   263→
   264→2. **Trackpad vs Mouse Detection:**
   265→   - Track event frequency with timestamps
   266→   - Events < 20ms apart = trackpad behavior
   267→   - After 3+ rapid events, mark as trackpad
   268→   - Large deltas (>50px) reset to mouse mode
   269→
   270→3. **Adaptive Sensitivity:**
   271→   - Mouse: MOUSE_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.003
   272→   - Trackpad: TRACKPAD_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.001 (lower for many small events)
   273→
   274→4. **Zoom Delta Clamping:**
   275→   - MAX_ZOOM_DELTA_PER_EVENT = 0.5
   276→   - Prevents jump zoom from large scroll events
   277→
   278→**Constants Added:**
   279→```typescript
   280→LINE_HEIGHT = 40                    // Pixels per line
   281→PAGE_HEIGHT = 800                   // Pixels per page
   282→MOUSE_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.003      // Base zoom sensitivity
   283→TRACKPAD_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.001   // Lower for trackpad
   284→MAX_ZOOM_DELTA_PER_EVENT = 0.5      // Cap per event
   285→TRACKPAD_DETECTION_THRESHOLD = 20   // ms between events
   286→TRACKPAD_EVENT_COUNT_THRESHOLD = 3  // Rapid events to confirm
   287→```

   305→### F118: Momentum/Inertia Panning - COMPLETE
   306→
   307→**Objective:**

   309→
   310→**Implementation:**
   311→
   312→1. **Velocity Tracking:**
   313→   - Track pointer position samples during drag with timestamps
   314→   - Store last N samples (5) for velocity calculation
   315→   - Calculate total displacement and time to get velocity
   316→
   317→2. **Momentum Animation:**
   318→   - On pointer release, calculate average velocity from samples
   319→   - Start `requestAnimationFrame` loop with initial velocity
   320→   - Apply friction (0.93) per frame to gradually slow down
   321→   - Stop when velocity < 0.5 world units
   322→
   323→3. **Integration:**
   324→   - Momentum stops on any user interaction (new drag, zoom)
   325→   - Cleanup animation on component unmount
   326→   - Camera position clamped to world bounds during animation

   331→MOMENTUM_MIN_VELOCITY = 0.5  // Stop threshold
   332→MOMENTUM_VELOCITY_SAMPLES = 5 // Samples to average

   869→## Session: 2025-12-16 (F105/F106 Architectural Fix - Camera Alignment)
   870→
   871→### Problem: Tasks Not Aligned with Sidebar
   872→
   873→**Symptoms:**
   874→- Tasks appeared in center-right of canvas instead of left edge
   875→- Tasks didn't align with train rows in sidebar
   876→- Camera could be dragged infinitely (no bounds)
   877→
   878→**Root Cause Analysis:**
   879→- InfiniteCanvas used `containerRef.getBoundingClientRect()` to measure canvas size
   880→- R3F's actual canvas size (from `useThree().size`) was different due to flexbox layout
   881→- Container div measured: 800x600
   882→- Actual R3F canvas: 1070x679
   883→- Camera was positioned incorrectly: at (400, -300) instead of (535, -339.5)
   884→- This caused viewport to show X from -135 to 935 instead of 0 to 1070
   885→
   886→**Solution:**
   887→- Created `CameraController` component that runs INSIDE the R3F Canvas context
   888→- Uses `useThree().size` to get actual canvas dimensions
   889→- Initializes camera position correctly on first render
   890→- Reports actual size back to parent for pan/zoom calculations
   891→
   892→**Files Changed:**
   893→- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` - Complete refactor
   894→  - Removed old `CameraSync` component
   895→  - Added new `CameraController` with proper R3F size handling
   896→  - Camera initialization now happens inside R3F context
   897→  - Pan/zoom handlers use correct canvas dimensions
   898→- `apps/canvas/src/data/types.ts` - Added helper functions
   899→  - `calculateDataBounds()` for computing task extent
   900→  - `calculateFitToView()` for D3.js-style fit-to-bounds
   901→
   902→**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
   903→- ✅ Tasks aligned with train rows (TRAIN-001 = first task row)
   904→- ✅ Tasks start near left edge of canvas
   905→- ✅ Pan bounded to world limits (can't drag infinitely)
   906→- ✅ Zoom works correctly (+/- buttons, wheel)
   907→- ✅ Reset View returns to initial state
   908→- ✅ Conflict indicators visible (red tasks)
   909→- ✅ No console errors
   910→
   911→**Key Insight:**
   912→When using R3F, always get canvas dimensions from `useThree().size`, not from the container div. The container and canvas can have different sizes due to CSS layout (flexbox, padding, borders).

# Metro Integrated Planning System - Progress Log

## Session: 2025-12-16 (Harness Tasks Creation) - COMPLETE ✅

### Novas Tarefas Adicionadas ao Harness

**Contexto:**
Usuário solicitou criação de várias tarefas importantes para melhorar o harness e a qualidade do código.

**Tarefas Criadas:**

1. **URGENT-001** (Priority 0 - EMERGENCIAL)
   - Migrar uso de React hooks para external stores em todo o projeto
   - React hooks causam problemas em WebGL: stale closures, batching, re-renders
   - External stores (useSyncExternalStore) resolvem todos esses problemas
   - Auditar código, documentar usos, migrar estados críticos
   - Atualizar CLAUDE.md com regra contra React hooks para estado crítico

2. **PERF-001** (Priority 0)
   - Adicionar ferramenta de métricas de performance WebGL em tempo real
   - Ferramentas pesquisadas: r3f-perf (recomendada), stats-gl, stats.js
   - Painel deve mostrar: FPS, MS, MB, GPU time, draw calls, triangles
   - Visível para AI (via screenshot) e humanos (no browser)
   - Toggle para mostrar/esconder (tecla 'P' ou botão)
   - **LOGGING DETALHADO** (adicionado após revisão):
     - Buffer circular em memória para últimos 60s de métricas
     - window.__PERF_LOG__ para acessar log via console
     - window.__PERF_EXPORT__() para exportar log completo
     - window.__PERF_START_SESSION__(name) / window.__PERF_STOP_SESSION__()
     - Estatísticas agregadas: FPS médio, p1, p99, frames abaixo de 30/60fps
     - AI pode usar browser_evaluate para análise programática
     - Documentar em docs/PERFORMANCE_LOGGING.md

3. **HARNESS-003** (Priority 1, blockedBy: PERF-001)
   - Atualizar protocolo de testes visuais para incluir métricas de performance
   - Métricas obrigatórias: FPS >= 60 idle, >= 30 interação; MS <= 16.67ms
   - **DUAS abordagens complementares**:
     - VISUAL: ler métricas do painel no screenshot (quick check)

## Session: 2025-12-16 (F122 - Fix elastic/jump drag bug) - COMPLETE ✅

### F122: Fix elastic/jump effect when dragging - COMPLETE

**Problem:**
Canvas felt elastic or jumped when starting a new drag during/after momentum animation.

**Root Cause Analysis:**
React state updates are batched/async. When user starts a new drag while momentum is running:
1. `handlePointerDown` captures `cameraPos` from React state
2. But React state hasn't been updated yet with latest momentum position
3. New drag starts from stale position → canvas "jumps" back

**Solution Implemented:**
1. Added `cameraPosRef` to track camera position synchronously via useRef
2. Created `updateCameraPos()` helper that updates BOTH ref and state
3. Updated all `setCameraPos()` calls to use `updateCameraPos()`:
   - `handleCameraInitialized`
   - `animateZoomTo` (zoom animation loop)
   - `startMomentum` (momentum animation loop)
   - `handlePinchZoom`
   - `handlePointerMove` (pan movement)
   - `handleDoubleClick` (double-click zoom animation)
4. Changed `handlePointerDown` to read from `cameraPosRef.current` instead of `cameraPos` state

**Files Changed:**
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (cameraPosRef, updateCameraPos helper)
- `features.json` (F122 passes: true)

### F121: Cross-platform scroll/input normalization - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Normalize scroll sensitivity across macOS/Windows/Linux and trackpad/mouse.

**Implementation:**

1. **deltaMode Normalization:**
   - LINE mode (deltaMode=1): multiply by LINE_HEIGHT (40px)
   - PAGE mode (deltaMode=2): multiply by PAGE_HEIGHT (800px)
   - PIXEL mode (deltaMode=0): use directly

2. **Trackpad vs Mouse Detection:**
   - Track event frequency with timestamps
   - Events < 20ms apart = trackpad behavior
   - After 3+ rapid events, mark as trackpad
   - Large deltas (>50px) reset to mouse mode

3. **Adaptive Sensitivity:**
   - Mouse: MOUSE_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.003
   - Trackpad: TRACKPAD_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.001 (lower for many small events)

4. **Zoom Delta Clamping:**
   - MAX_ZOOM_DELTA_PER_EVENT = 0.5
   - Prevents jump zoom from large scroll events

**Constants Added:**
```typescript
LINE_HEIGHT = 40                    // Pixels per line
PAGE_HEIGHT = 800                   // Pixels per page
MOUSE_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.003      // Base zoom sensitivity
TRACKPAD_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.001   // Lower for trackpad
MAX_ZOOM_DELTA_PER_EVENT = 0.5      // Cap per event
TRACKPAD_DETECTION_THRESHOLD = 20   // ms between events
TRACKPAD_EVENT_COUNT_THRESHOLD = 3  // Rapid events to confirm
```

## Session: 2025-12-16 (F118 - Momentum Panning) - COMPLETE ✅

### F118: Momentum/Inertia Panning - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Implement velocity-based drag with momentum/inertia like Google Maps and iOS.

**Implementation:**

1. **Velocity Tracking:**
   - Track pointer position samples during drag with timestamps
   - Store last N samples (5) for velocity calculation
   - Calculate total displacement and time to get velocity

2. **Momentum Animation:**
   - On pointer release, calculate average velocity from samples
   - Start `requestAnimationFrame` loop with initial velocity
   - Apply friction (0.93) per frame to gradually slow down
   - Stop when velocity < 0.5 world units

3. **Integration:**
   - Momentum stops on any user interaction (new drag, zoom)
   - Cleanup animation on component unmount
   - Camera position clamped to world bounds during animation

**Constants Used:**
```typescript
MOMENTUM_FRICTION = 0.93     // Per-frame velocity multiplier
MOMENTUM_MIN_VELOCITY = 0.5  // Stop threshold
MOMENTUM_VELOCITY_SAMPLES = 5 // Samples to average
```

## Session: 2025-12-16 (F105/F106 Architectural Fix - Camera Alignment)

### Problem: Tasks Not Aligned with Sidebar

**Symptoms:**
- Tasks appeared in center-right of canvas instead of left edge
- Tasks didn't align with train rows in sidebar
- Camera could be dragged infinitely (no bounds)

**Root Cause Analysis:**
- InfiniteCanvas used `containerRef.getBoundingClientRect()` to measure canvas size
- R3F's actual canvas size (from `useThree().size`) was different due to flexbox layout
- Container div measured: 800x600
- Actual R3F canvas: 1070x679
- Camera was positioned incorrectly: at (400, -300) instead of (535, -339.5)
- This caused viewport to show X from -135 to 935 instead of 0 to 1070

**Solution:**
- Created `CameraController` component that runs INSIDE the R3F Canvas context
- Uses `useThree().size` to get actual canvas dimensions
- Initializes camera position correctly on first render
- Reports actual size back to parent for pan/zoom calculations

**Files Changed:**
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` - Complete refactor
  - Removed old `CameraSync` component
  - Added new `CameraController` with proper R3F size handling
  - Camera initialization now happens inside R3F context
  - Pan/zoom handlers use correct canvas dimensions
- `apps/canvas/src/data/types.ts` - Added helper functions
  - `calculateDataBounds()` for computing task extent
  - `calculateFitToView()` for D3.js-style fit-to-bounds

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ Tasks aligned with train rows (TRAIN-001 = first task row)
- ✅ Tasks start near left edge of canvas
- ✅ Pan bounded to world limits (can't drag infinitely)
- ✅ Zoom works correctly (+/- buttons, wheel)
- ✅ Reset View returns to initial state
- ✅ Conflict indicators visible (red tasks)
- ✅ No console errors

**Key Insight:**
When using R3F, always get canvas dimensions from `useThree().size`, not from the container div. The container and canvas can have different sizes due to CSS layout (flexbox, padding, borders).

**Features Updated:**
- F105: ✅ Passes (tasks aligned with sidebar)
- F106: ✅ Passes (pan/zoom with correct bounds)
- F114: ✅ Passes (conflict indicators already working)

### Issue: R3F or Three.js import errors
**Solution**: Use `bun add` to install packages, never edit package.json manually

### Issue: Astro hydration issues with React
**Solution**: Always use `client:only="react"` for R3F components

### Issue: Shader compilation errors
**Solution**: Check GLSL syntax, verify uniform names match

**Solution**: Use usePlanningSelector() instead of usePlanning() for type safety
